questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Select the antonym of 'TRANSPARENT'.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** 'Transparent' means allowing light to pass through so objects are visible.

**Working.** The opposite is 'opaque' (not allowing light through); the other options are near-synonyms of transparent.

**Answer.** opaque.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Convert $72$ km/h into m/s.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Multiply km/h by $\tfrac{5}{18}$ to get m/s.

**Working.** $72\times\dfrac{5}{18}=20$ m/s.

**Answer.** $20$ m/s.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A can finish a job in $12$ days and B in $6$ days. Working together, they finish it in _____ days.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Add the per-day work rates.

**Working.** $\dfrac{1}{12}+\dfrac{1}{6}=\dfrac{1}{12}+\dfrac{2}{12}=\dfrac{3}{12}=\dfrac{1}{4}$, so $4$ days.

**Answer.** $4$ days.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The square root of $196$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Find the number whose square is $196$.

**Working.** $14^2 = 196$.

**Answer.** $14$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: On the clock face shown, the time is $3{:}00$. Compute the smaller angle between the hour and minute hands (degrees).

figure.caption: Clock at 3:00
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 170 175' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><circle cx='85' cy='80' r='60' fill='#e5e7eb' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='85' y1='80' x2='85' y2='28' stroke='#1f2937' stroke-width='3'/><line x1='85' y1='80' x2='133' y2='80' stroke='#dc2626' stroke-width='2'/><text x='55' y='160' font-size='8'>time = 3:00</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Each hour mark is $30^\circ$ apart.

**Working.**
$$3\text{ hours}\times30^\circ=90^\circ$$

**Answer.** $90^\circ$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Express the right side as a power of the same base.

**Working.** $32=2^5$, so $x=5$.

**Answer.** $5$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: At sunrise, the shadow of a vertical pole falls toward which direction?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A shadow points away from the sun.

**Working.** At sunrise the sun is in the east, so the shadow falls toward the west.

**Answer.** West.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A company's headcount by department is A $30$, B $50$, C $20$ (see bar chart). Department B's share of the total workforce is _____ % .

figure.caption: Bar chart of department headcounts: A 30, B 50, C 20.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='30' y1='160' x2='200' y2='160' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><rect x='50' y='100' width='30' height='60' fill='#6366f1'/><rect x='100' y='60' width='30' height='100' fill='#10b981'/><rect x='150' y='120' width='30' height='40' fill='#f59e0b'/><text x='60' y='173' font-size='10' fill='#94a3b8'>A</text><text x='110' y='173' font-size='10' fill='#94a3b8'>B</text><text x='160' y='173' font-size='10' fill='#94a3b8'>C</text></svg>

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: An article sold for $480$ incurs a loss of $20\%$. Its cost price is

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Choose the correctly spelt and appropriate word: 'The new regulation will ____ the older guidelines.'
questions.solution:
**Concept.** 'Supersede' (to replace and render obsolete) is the correct word and spelling.

**Working.** 'Supercede' is a misspelling; 'surpass' means to exceed, and 'suppress' means to subdue — neither fits 'replace guidelines'.

**Answer.** supersede.

questions.subject: Mineralogy & Crystallography

questions.subject: Metamorphic Petrology
questions.stem: For a geothermal gradient of $30\,^\circ$C/km, the temperature at $25\,$km (surface $0\,^\circ$C) is _____ $^\circ$C.

questions.subject: Stratigraphy & Indian Geology
questions.stem: A $500\,$m sequence accumulated at $10\,$m/Myr. The time represented is _____ Myr.

questions.subject: Structural Geology
questions.stem: A planar fault strikes $60^\circ$ and dips $35^\circ$. The plunge of the true-dip line (steepest line on the plane) is _____ degrees.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The true-dip line plunges at the dip angle, in the dip direction.

**Working.**
$$\text{plunge}=\text{dip}=35^\circ.$$

**Answer.** $35^\circ$

questions.subject: Metamorphic Petrology
questions.stem: A non-foliated, fine-grained rock formed by contact metamorphism of pelite is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Contact (thermal) metamorphism of mudrock yields hornfels (non-foliated).

**Working.**
Hornfels.

**Answer.** Hornfels

questions.subject: Igneous Petrology
questions.stem: The volcanic (fine-grained) equivalent of granite is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Granite (plutonic felsic) ↔ rhyolite (volcanic felsic).

**Working.**
Rhyolite.

**Answer.** Rhyolite

questions.subject: Igneous Petrology
questions.stem: A plagioclase has An$_{20}$. Its series index (0=albite…5=anorthite, by 20% steps An0/10/30/50/70/90) is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Albite(0–10), oligoclase(10–30), andesine(30–50), labradorite(50–70), bytownite(70–90), anorthite(90–100).

**Working.**
$$An_{20}\Rightarrow 1.$$

**Answer.** $1$
